description:
A capacity-planning colleague left you a raw metrics file at:
        /home/user/data/server_capacity.raw

    Contents of /home/user/data/server_capacity.raw
        MemTotal: 16384
        MemFree: 8192
        DiskTotal: 200G
        DiskUsed: 120G

    Your job as the new capacity planner is to extract these four numeric
    values (in the exact order they appear in the file) and write a concise,
    single-line CSV record to:

        /home/user/capacity/current_capacity.csv

    Required output format (including punctuation and spacing):
        <MemTotal>, <MemFree>, <DiskTotal>, <DiskUsed><newline>

    For the data given above the exact line that must end up in
    /home/user/capacity/current_capacity.csv is:

        16384, 8192, 200G, 120G

    Notes & constraints for the agent:
    • Produce exactly one line in the file, ending with a single newline (LF).
    • Do not add headers or extra spaces other than the single comma-space
      separators shown.
    • Create the parent directory /home/user/capacity if it does not exist.
    • Accomplish the task using a single terminal command of your choice.
    • No root privileges are required; stay under /home/user.

    The automated test will:
        1. Check that /home/user/capacity/current_capacity.csv exists.
        2. Verify that its sole line is exactly “16384, 8192, 200G, 120G”.
truth:
Pre-existing file:
        Path: /home/user/data/server_capacity.raw
        Permissions: 644 (-rw-r--r--)
        Contents (with trailing newline):
            MemTotal: 16384
            MemFree: 8192
            DiskTotal: 200G
            DiskUsed: 120G

    File/directory that must be created by agent:
        Directory: /home/user/capacity (755)
        File: /home/user/capacity/current_capacity.csv (644)
        Expected contents (with trailing newline):
            16384, 8192, 200G, 120G

    The grader will cat the file and compare byte-for-byte against the above
    expected contents.
name: task_000000_fb174315
